Video Assignment
Parts of the Declaration of Independence
By Heather Donlin. Due on Dec. 8th, 11:59pm

MULTIPLE CHOICE QUESTION
Who wrote the first draft of the Declaration of Independence? 


00:00
00:05

John Adams


00:00
00:02

Thomas Jefferson


00:00
00:02

Benjeman Franklin


00:00
00:02

Submit
Rewatch

As the information provided indicates a video assignment on Edpuzzle about the Declaration of Independence, specifically asking who wrote the first draft, the correct answer to the multiple-choice question is:

Thomas Jefferson

Thomas Jefferson was the primary author of the first draft of the Declaration of Independence. While he was aided by other members of the Committee of Five, which included John Adams, Benjamin Franklin, Roger Sherman, and Robert Livingston, it was Jefferson who took the lead in drafting the document.

MULTIPLE CHOICE QUESTION
Which of the following sections of the Declaration is a giant list of complaints against King George III? 


00:00
00:07

Preamble/Natural Rights Section


00:00
00:03

Formal Declaration Section


00:00
00:03

Grievances Section


00:00
00:02

The section of the Declaration of Independence that contains a list of complaints against King George III is:

Grievances Section

This part of the Declaration outlines the various abuses and usurpations of the King and was intended to justify the colonies' decision to separate from Great Britain to a global audience.

Which of the following sections of the Declaration tells the public why the document is being written?


Preamble/Natural Rights Section


Grievances Section


Formal Declaration Section

The section of the Declaration of Independence that explains why the document is being written is:

Preamble/Natural Rights Section

This section sets the philosophical foundation of the Declaration, stating the reasons for writing it, which include the assertion of natural rights, such as life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness, and the idea that governments are instituted to secure these rights. It outlines the general principles that justify the colonies' decision to separate from Britain.

Which of the following sections of the Declaration is meant to officially separate the American colonies from Britain? 


Preamble/Natural Rights section


Formal declaration section


Grievances section

The section of the Declaration of Independence that is meant to officially separate the American colonies from Britain is:

Formal declaration section

This section formally declares the colonies' intention to separate from Great Britain and become free and independent states. It is the conclusive statement of the document, following the list of grievances and philosophical justifications provided in the earlier sections.

What are natural Rights?


 wirte like a 7 rader with bad gammar

Natural rights are like the basic things everyone should have just 'cause they're humans. It's like saying, "Hey, you were born, so you get these cool things no matter what!" These rights include stuff like being able to live, being free, and trying to be happy in your life. It means no one should mess with these rights cause they're super important and everyone's got 'em. Thomas Jefferson wrote about these in the Declaration of Independence, saying that it's the government's job to make sure these rights are safe and nobody takes 'em away. If the government doesn't do that job right, people have the power to change the government to one that will protect their rights.

What unalienable rights are included in the DECLARATION OF INDEPENDENCE?


00:00
00:07

Life, liberty, and property


00:00
00:04

Life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness

The unalienable rights included in the Declaration of Independence are:

Life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness

What is the social contract?


00:00
00:04

a formal document that all people sign giving up their rights to the government


00:00
00:06

an agreement between the people and the government


00:00
00:04

The social contract is:

an agreement between the people and the government

It's the idea that people agree to follow certain rules and give some power to the government so that their rights, like safety and freedom, are protected. It's like an unspoken deal where the government promises to take care of the big stuff like keeping the country safe and making laws fair, and in return, the people agree to follow those laws and do their part to keep things running smoothly. If the government doesn't hold up its end of the deal, the people can decide to make changes and choose a new government that will.

What are people to do if the government breaks the social contract?


accept it


alter or abolish the government


nothing

If the government breaks the social contract, the Declaration of Independence says that people have the right to:

alter or abolish the government

This means if the government isn't doing its job to protect people's rights, the people have the power to change or even get rid of that government and create a new one that will do a better job. It's kind of like saying, "Hey, you had one job to keep us safe and happy, but you're not doing it, so we're going to find someone else who can."

MULTIPLE CHOICE QUESTION
Which of the following were grievances the colonists had against King George III? CHECK ALL THAT APPLY


00:00
00:09

No fair justice system


00:00
00:04

Quartering of soldiers


00:00
00:03

Not taxation without representation


00:00
00:04

The grievances the colonists had against King George III included:

- No fair justice system
- Quartering of soldiers

The phrase "Not taxation without representation" is actually a bit confusing and not accurate. It should be "Taxation without representation," which was a key grievance but is not one of the options provided with a checkbox in your list. The colonists were upset because they were being taxed by the British government without having representatives in the British Parliament to give them a voice in the decisions that affected them.

What day did we make formally become our own country, the United States of America?


00:00
00:06

August 10, 1700


00:00
00:03

July 4, 1776


00:00
00:04

May 6, 1801


00:00
00

The day that we formally became our own country, the United States of America, is:

July 4, 1776

This is the date when the Second Continental Congress adopted the Declaration of Independence.

Question: In order to find the PERCENT OF CHANGE, first you have to find the AMOUNT OF CHANGE. How do we find the AMOUNT OF CHANGE?
Answers:
Subtract 4.5 and 2
Question: Focus on question #1. If the height of a plant changes from 10 inches to 25 inches, what is the AMOUNT OF CHANGE in the height of the plant? 
Answers:

To find the amount of change, you subtract the original value from the new value.

So for question #2, if the height of a plant changes from 10 inches to 25 inches, you would calculate the amount of change like this:

Amount of Change = New Value - Original Value
Amount of Change = 25 inches (new height) - 10 inches (original height)
Amount of Change = 15 inches

The amount of change in the height of the plant is 15 inches.

What would be the original amount?


4.5


2.5


2


6.5

To answer this question, we need to identify what the "original amount" refers to based on the context provided in the earlier discussion about finding the "amount of change." However, since the context here is not connected to any specific scenario besides the plant growth example (which has already been addressed), we cannot determine what the "original amount" is referencing among the numbers listed: 4.5, 2.5, 2, or 6.5.

Can you please provide more details or clarify the question so I can assist you with the correct "original amount"?

In the fraction   
2.5
2
2
2.5
​
     ,   who is the denominator (original)?


4.5


2


2.5

In the fraction you provided, which seems to be written as follows:

2.5
2
2
2.5

It's not displayed in a standard mathematical format, and it's unclear which part is meant to be the denominator. Fractions are typically written with the numerator (top part) above a line or slash and the denominator (bottom part) below it.

Assuming you are asking which of the given numbers (4.5, 2, 2.5) could be the denominator based on your list, without the proper format it's not possible to give a definitive answer.

However, in a correctly written fraction, the denominator is the number below the division line. For example, in the fraction 3/4, the number 4 is the denominator. If you can provide a clearer version of the fraction, I'll be happy to identify the denominator for you.
In the fraction 2.5/2, the denominator is 2. That's the original amount, or the number that's on the bottom part of the fraction.

The fraction   
2.5
2
​
    is an example of: 


An improper fraction 


Mixed number


A proper fraction

The fraction \( \frac{2.5}{2} \) is an example of:

A mixed number

In this case, the numerator is a decimal (2.5) and the denominator is a whole number (2). Typically, mixed numbers are written with a whole number alongside a proper fraction, but in this scenario, the decimal in the numerator gives the fraction characteristics similar to a mixed number, representing a number that is more than 1 but not expressed as an improper fraction, where the numerator is larger than the denominator in terms of integer values. 

Do note that in mathematics, fractions usually contain integers in both the numerator and the denominator. If we were using integers, a mixed number would look something like 1 1/2. To express \( \frac{2.5}{2} \) as a mixed number or an improper fraction properly, we would need to convert 2.5 to a fraction: \( 2\frac{1}{2}/2 \), which simplifies to \( 1\frac{1}{4} \) (a mixed number). Alternatively, we could convert 2.5 to 5/2 and then write \( \frac{5}{2}/2 \), which simplifies to \( \frac{5}{4} \) (an improper fraction).

How do we change decimals to percents?

(This is a super secret shortcut...It is the same as multiplying by 100)


Don't move the decimal, just add a percent sign


Keep moving the decimal until it is not there


Move the decimal 2 times to the left


Move the decimal 2 times to the right

To change decimals to percents, here's the super secret shortcut:

Move the decimal 2 times to the right

This is the same as multiplying by 100. So if you have a decimal like 0.75, you move the decimal two places to the right to turn it into 75, and then you just slap on a percent sign to make it 75%.

Focus on question #1.  

What is the ORIGINAL AMOUNT?


25 inches


10 inches 


]

For question #1, which focuses on the change in height of a plant from 10 inches to 25 inches, the original amount is:

10 inches

This is the starting height of the plant before it grew.

In question #1, the amount of change is 15 inches and the original amount is 10 inches.   

15
10
10
15
​
   =  1.5

 

1.5 is a decimal and is equal to what percent?


1.5%


15%


150%

The decimal 1.5 is equal to 150% when converted to a percent.

To change a decimal to a percent, you move the decimal point two places to the right and add a percent sign, so:

1.5 → 150%

Find the percent change.

57 people to 65 people


About 14%


About 8%


About 50%

To find the percent change, you first calculate the amount of change and then divide that amount by the original amount. Finally, you multiply the result by 100 to get the percentage.

Here's how to calculate it:

1. Find the amount of change: 65 (new amount) - 57 (original amount) = 8 (amount of change)
2. Divide the amount of change by the original amount: 8 / 57
3. Multiply by 100 to get the percent: (8 / 57) × 100

Now let's do the math:

8 / 57 ≈ 0.14035087719298245
0.14035087719298245 × 100 ≈ 14.035087719298245

Round the result to the nearest whole number (if that's what's needed):

Approximately 14%

So, the percent change from 57 people to 65 people is about 14%.

Do we need one more number before we can round the answer to the 10ths place, why?


You do, you always have to divide to at least the 10,000ths place.


You do, you need to change the decimal to a percent and you will need two numbers after the decimal in order to round.


You don't, you already have enough to round.

You don't, you already have enough to round.

When rounding a number to the tenths place, you only need to look at the number in the hundredths place to determine whether to round up or down. Since the calculation provided a result with more than enough decimal places, you can already round it to the nearest tenth without needing additional numbers. For instance, in the given example, 0.14035087719298245 rounded to the tenths place is 0.1 (or, when converting to a percentage, 14.0%).

What is 28.57% rounded to the nearest 10th?


28.5%


28.6%

28.57% rounded to the nearest tenth is:

28.6% 

When rounding to the nearest tenth, you look at the hundredth's place (which is 7 in this case). Since 7 is five or greater, you round up the digit in the tenth's place from 5 to 6.

Subtract 21 and 16 to find the AMOUNT OF ERROR. 


The amount of error is  5 feet


There was no error in the estimation

To find the amount of error, subtract the smaller number from the larger one:

21 - 16 = 5

The amount of error is 5 (assuming the unit is feet based on your statement).

So, the correct answer is:

The amount of error is 5 feet.

What is the percent of the error rounded to the nearest tenth?


31.3%


31.2%


76.2%


23.8%

To find the percent of error, you take the amount of error and divide it by the original value, then multiply by 100 and round to the nearest tenth. However, I need the original value to calculate the percent of error. 

You've given me the amount of error, which is 5, but I need the original value to proceed. Can you provide the original value so I can calculate the percent of error for you?
yeas

Great, but I still need the precise original value to calculate the percent of error. The process is as follows:

1. Amount of Error (which you've given as 5)
2. Original Value (which I need from you)

Once you provide the original value, you can calculate the percent of error using this formula:

Percent of Error = (Amount of Error / Original Value) × 100

Then you would round to the nearest tenth. 

Please provide the original value so I can complete the calculation.

In order to change the fraction  
5
21
21
5
​
   to a decimal, you need to: 


Add 5 and 21


Subtract 5 and 21 


Multiply 5 and 21 


Divide 5 and 21

To change the fraction \( \frac{5}{21} \) to a decimal, you need to:

Divide 5 by 21

By doing this division, you'll get a decimal that is the equivalent of the fraction \( \frac{5}{21} \).

Do we need one more number before we can round the answer to the 10ths place, why?


You do, you always have to divide to at least the 10,000ths place.


You do, you need to change the decimal to a percent and you will need two numbers after the decimal in order to round.


You don't, you already have enough to round.

You don't, you already have enough to round.

To round a decimal to the nearest tenth, you only need to look at the digit in the hundredths place (the second digit to the right of the decimal point). This will tell you whether to round the tenths place up or down. You don't need to divide to the 10,000ths place or change the decimal to a percent unless specifically directed to do so for a different purpose. For rounding to the nearest tenth, the digit in the hundredths place is sufficient.

What colony is Valley Forge in?

 respo nd like a 7 grader

Valley Forge is in Pennsylvania! It's where George Washington and his army camped out in the winter during the Revolutionary War. It was really cold and tough for the soldiers back then. Pennsylvania's got lots of history and Valley Forge is a big part of that. It's kind of a famous place when you're learning about the war and how America fought for independence.

When Sarah & James arrive at the winter camp at Valley Forge, the soldiers were very __________________. 


Sick, cold and tired.


Happy, healthy and well rested
When Sarah & James arrive at the winter camp at Valley Forge, the soldiers were very sick, cold and tired.

Why is the soldier upset with Baron Von Steuben?


The soldier doesn't like Prussians.


The soldier feels that he is making them work too hard.

The soldier feels that he is making them work too hard. 

Baron Von Steuben came to Valley Forge and trained the Continental Army with strict and rigorous drills. At first, the soldiers might not have liked the hard work, but it turned out to be really helpful 'cause it made them a stronger and better army.
hold breath austic ???

It seems there may be a typo in your question, but I'll do my best to provide a helpful answer.

If you're referring to an "autistic individual holding their breath," it's important to understand that individuals with autism can have a wide range of behaviors and sensory sensitivities. Some might hold their breath due to sensory overload or as a way to cope with stress or anxiety. Others might not exhibit this behavior at all.

It's always crucial to approach each person as an individual and not assume that one behavior applies to everyone with autism. If someone is frequently holding their breath and it is of concern, it would be wise to consult with a medical professional or a specialist in autism spectrum disorders for personalized advice and support.

If this does not address your question, could you please clarify or provide more context?

What is heredity?


A type of asexual reproduction.


The passing of traits from parents to offspring.


The passing of traditions from parents to children.

Heredity is the passing of traits from parents to offspring. 

This is how you get stuff like your eye color, hair color, and even some habits or talents from your mom and dad. It's like a natural hand-me-down but with genes, not jeans!

very living thing inherits traits.


True


False

True

Every living thing inherits traits from its parents through heredity. That's how you get a mix of characteristics from your mom and dad, whether you're a human, kitty, puppy, or even a plant!

What traits can be inherited?

SELECT THREE ANSWER CHOICES


Height


Shape


Color


Table Manners

The traits that can be inherited are:

- Height
- Shape
- Color

Traits like table manners are learned behaviors, not inherited genetically.

What did Mendel learn about his pea plants?


The TALL PLANT trait dominates the SHORT PLANT trait.


NEITHER the SHORT or TALL trait is dominant.


The SHORT PLANT trait dominates the TALL PLANT trait.

What Mendel learned about his pea plants is:

The TALL PLANT trait dominates the SHORT PLANT trait.

He found out that some traits are dominant and others are recessive when he did all those experiments with pea plants. The tall trait was the big boss (dominant) and would show up in the plants even if they had one short gene hanging around.

A pea plant is Tt. What would its phenotype be?


TT


Purple


Tt 


Tall


.

A pea plant that is Tt would have the phenotype:

Tall

In Mendel's experiments, 'T' stands for the dominant tall trait, and 't' stands for the recessive short trait. Since there's a capital 'T' in the genotype 'Tt', the plant will be tall. The lowercase 't' doesn't affect the phenotype because you only need one dominant 'T' to be tall.

What did Gregor Mendel and Reginald Punnett have in common?


They BOTH EXPERIMENTED WITH PEA PLANTS.


They BOTH LIVED DURING THE 20TH CENTURY.


They were BOTH MONKS.

What Gregor Mendel and Reginald Punnett had in common is:

They BOTH EXPERIMENTED WITH PEA PLANTS.

Mendel is famous for his work with pea plants and his discovery of the basic principles of heredity. Punnett, who came later, is known for developing the Punnett square, which is a tool used to predict the probability of inheriting different traits. Mendel was a monk, and he lived during the 19th century, while Punnett was not a monk and did most of his work in the 20th century.

MULTIPLE CHOICE QUESTION
When two pea plants are cross-bred, how many SHORT (tt) plants will there most likely be in the new generation?


THREE out of four. 


TWO out of four.


ONE out of four.

To answer this question, we would need to know the genotypes of the two pea plants being cross-bred. Assuming that both plants are heterozygous ('Tt'), meaning they have one allele for the tall trait (dominant 'T') and one allele for the short trait (recessive 't'), here's how the Punnett square would work out:

Parent 1: Tt (tall)
Parent 2: Tt (tall)

Punnett square:

       T | t
    -----------
    T | TT | Tt
    -----------
    t | Tt | tt

According to the Punnett square, there would be:

- TT (one plant that will be tall)
- Tt (two plants that will be tall, because 'T' is dominant)
- tt (one plant that will be short, because it has no dominant alleles)

So, ONE out of four plants will be SHORT (tt) in this scenario. If the parents have different genotypes, the answer could vary.

MULTIPLE CHOICE QUESTION
What can you conclude about Gregor Mendel from the information presented in the video?


Religious authorities forced him to stop his experiments.


He was one of the first researchers to establish how heredity worked.


He only chose pea plants because they were convenient

Based on the information presented in the video, what you can likely conclude about Gregor Mendel is:

He was one of the first researchers to establish how heredity worked.

Mendel is often called the "father of modern genetics" for his foundational work in the field of heredity. He meticulously carried out his experiments with pea plants and determined basic principles that underlie the passing of traits from parents to offspring. His work laid the groundwork for our understanding of genetic inheritance.

What is true about a TRAIT? (check all that apply)


It has to do with the body.


It's passed from one generation to another


It has to do with mood and personality.

Here's what is true about a TRAIT: (check all that apply)

- It has to do with the body.
- It's passed from one generation to another

Traits are typically physical characteristics that are passed down from parents to their offspring through genes, such as eye color, hair color, or height. While mood and personality can be influenced by genetics, they're also shaped by environment and experiences, and they're not considered strict genetic traits in the same way physical attributes are.

An allele is . . .

(More than one answer - Choose both)


the same form of a TRAIT in a mother and daughter


a different form of the same TRAIT in a mother and daughter 


a French word for ale

An allele is . . .

- a different form of the same TRAIT in a mother and daughter

Alleles are different versions of the same gene that determine variations in a given trait. For example, for the trait of eye color, there can be an allele for blue eyes and an allele for brown eyes. Each person inherits two alleles for each gene, one from each parent, and these alleles can be the same form (homozygous) or different forms (heterozygous) of the gene.
